en: The wicked flee when no one pursues, but the righteous are bold as a lion.
zh: 惡人雖然沒有人追趕，仍然逃跑； 義人卻像獅子，放膽無懼。

en: When a land transgresses, it has many rulers, but with a man of understanding and knowledge, its stability will long continue.
zh: 國家因有過犯，領袖就經常更換； 依賴聰明知識俱備的人，國家才可以長存。

en: A poor man who oppresses the poor is a beating rain that leaves no food.
zh: 窮人欺壓貧寒人， 就像暴雨沖沒糧食。

en: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, but those who keep the law strive against them.
zh: 離棄律法的，稱讚惡人； 遵守律法的，卻與惡人相爭。

en: Evil men do not understand justice, but those who seek the LORD understand it completely.
zh: 壞人不知公正是甚麼， 尋求耶和華的卻完全明白。

en: Better is a poor man who walks in his integrity than a rich man who is crooked in his ways.
zh: 行為完全的窮人， 勝過行事狡詐的財主。

en: The one who keeps the law is a son with understanding, but a companion of gluttons shames his father.
zh: 謹守訓誨的，是聰明的人； 與貪食的人作伴的，卻使父親蒙羞。

en: Whoever multiplies his wealth by interest and profit gathers it for him who is generous to the poor.
zh: 凡是藉著高息厚利增加自己財富的， 就是為那恩待窮人的人積蓄。

en: If one turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.
zh: 轉身不聽訓誨的， 他的禱告也是可厭惡的。

en: Whoever misleads the upright into an evil way will fall into his own pit, but the blameless will have a goodly inheritance.
zh: 誘惑正直人走上邪路的， 必掉在自己所挖的坑裡； 完全人卻必承受福氣。

en: A rich man is wise in his own eyes, but a poor man who has understanding will find him out.
zh: 財主自以為有智慧， 聰明的窮人卻能看透他。

en: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory, but when the wicked rise, people hide themselves.
zh: 義人得勝的時候，有極大的榮耀； 惡人興起的時候，人就躲避。

en: Whoever conceals his transgressions will not prosper, but he who confesses and forsakes them will obtain mercy.
zh: 遮掩自己過犯的，必不亨通； 承認並離棄過犯的，必蒙憐憫。

en: Blessed is the one who fears the LORD always, but whoever hardens his heart will fall into calamity.
zh: 常存戒懼之心的，這人就為有福； 心裡剛硬的，必陷在禍患裡。

en: Like a roaring lion or a charging bear is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
zh: 殘暴的統治者轄制貧民， 就像吼叫的獅子，又像飢餓覓食的熊。

en: A ruler who lacks understanding is a cruel oppressor, but he who hates unjust gain will prolong his days.
zh: 昏庸的君主必多行強暴， 憎惡不義之財的，必享長壽。

en: If one is burdened with the blood of another, he will be a fugitive until death; let no one help him.
zh: 背負流人血之罪的， 必逃跑至死，人不可幫助他。

en: Whoever walks in integrity will be delivered, but he who is crooked in his ways will suddenly fall.
zh: 行為完全的，必蒙拯救； 行事狡詐的，必立刻跌倒。

en: Whoever works his land will have plenty of bread, but he who follows worthless pursuits will have plenty of poverty.
zh: 耕種自己田地的，必有充足糧食； 追求虛幻的，必飽受窮乏之苦。

en: A faithful man will abound with blessings, but whoever hastens to be rich will not go unpunished.
zh: 忠實的人必有大福； 急於發財的，不免受罰。

en: To show partiality is not good, but for a piece of bread a man will do wrong.
zh: 看人的情面是不對的； 但有人為了一點食物，竟然犯法。

en: A stingy man hastens after wealth and does not know that poverty will come upon him.
zh: 吝嗇的人急切求財 ， 卻不知窮乏快要臨到。

en: Whoever rebukes a man will afterward find more favor than he who flatters with his tongue.
zh: 責備人的，終必得人喜悅， 勝過那用舌頭諂媚人的。

en: Whoever robs his father or his mother and says, "That is no transgression," is a companion to a man who destroys.
zh: 偷竊自己父母財物， 並說：“這不是罪過”的， 這人是與強盜一夥。

en: A greedy man stirs up strife, but the one who trusts in the LORD will be enriched.
zh: 貪心的人，必引起紛爭； 倚靠耶和華的，必得豐裕。

en: Whoever trusts in his own mind is a fool, but he who walks in wisdom will be delivered.
zh: 自恃聰明的，是愚昧人； 憑著智慧行事的，必蒙拯救。

en: Whoever gives to the poor will not want, but he who hides his eyes will get many a curse.
zh: 賙濟窮人的，必不致缺乏； 閉眼不理他們的，必多受咒詛。

en: When the wicked rise, people hide themselves, but when they perish, the righteous increase.
zh: 惡人興起的時候，人就躲避； 惡人滅亡的時候，義人就增多。
